title: Ghostbusters
logline: Three unemployed parapsychology professors start a ghost-catching business in New York City, only to discover that an ancient Sumerian god is planning to use a Manhattan apartment building as a gateway to destroy the world.
protagonist: Dr. Peter Venkman (with ensemble support from Stantz and Spengler)
antagonistOrConflict: Gozer the Gozerian, an ancient Sumerian deity attempting to cross into our dimension through Dana Barrett's apartment building
tone: Irreverent, fast-paced, blending deadpan humor with genuine supernatural horror and spectacle

actBreaks.description: The Ghostbusters officially open for business at the firehouse, transitioning from academic outcasts to professional paranormal investigators.

validation.ruling: Scene #31 shows the Ghostbusters setting up their business, which is the FIRST scene of Act 2, not the last scene of Act 1. Scene #30 (p20, 21.3%) is the correct Act 1 break: Dana experiences the terrifying supernatural event in her kitchen where eggs explode and a demonic voice roars her name from the refrigerator. This irreversible moment forces her to seek help from the Ghostbusters, fundamentally changing the story direction and raising the stakes from academic curiosity to real danger.

actBreaks.description: The EPA shuts down the containment unit, releasing all captured ghosts and triggering the apocalyptic endgame with Dana and Louis' full possession.

validation.ruling: Scene #99 (p69, 73.4%) is the correct Act 2 break: the storage facility explodes, releasing all captured ghosts in a massive energy geyser. This catastrophic event is irreversible, raises stakes to city-wide apocalyptic levels, and forces the Ghostbusters into the final confrontation with Gozer. The story cannot return to its previous state of successful ghost-catching business.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script follows a classic three-act entrepreneurial rise structure with a supernatural twist. Act One establishes the academic world, introduces the ghost phenomenon, and ends with the team going into business. Act Two chronicles their meteoric success through a montage-heavy middle section (scenes 60-67) before introducing the romantic subplot with Dana and building toward the regulatory conflict with Peck. The act break is explosive—literally—as the containment breach transforms a business comedy into an apocalyptic showdown. Act Three is pure escalation: arrest, mayoral intervention, and the climactic rooftop battle. The structure is remarkably efficient, using New York itself as a character and maintaining momentum through rapid scene turnover.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The script moves at breakneck speed with an average scene length of just 1.2 pages. The opening library sequence (scenes 1-12) establishes tone and stakes quickly. The middle section accelerates through a montage of success (scenes 60-67, covering multiple locations in rapid succession). The Hotel Sedgewick sequence (scenes 38-59) is the script's longest sustained set piece, taking 21 scenes to build the first major ghost capture. The final act maintains relentless forward momentum from the containment breach (scene 99) through the climax, with no breathing room—pure escalation to the Stay-Puft Man reveal and resolution.

incitingIncident.description: The team observes an ethereal presence forming into an elderly woman between the stacks, and Venkman awkwardly attempts to communicate before the ghost turns hostile and chases them.

validation.ruling: Scene #2 is too early and passive (just showing a librarian in a reading room). Scene #12 (p9, 9.6%) is the true inciting incident: the librarian encounters the ghost in the library stacks, which directly leads to the Ghostbusters being called to investigate their first case, shattering their academic status quo and forcing them into action as paranormal investigators.

midpoint.description: Dana enters her apartment exhausted, sits down, removes her leg warmers, and answers a phone call from her mother reassuring her that everything is fine.

validation.ruling: Scene #69 occurs at 56.4% but is a passive setup scene where Dana simply comes home tired. Scene #59 (p40, 42.6%) is the true midpoint: the Ghostbusters successfully capture their first ghost at the Hotel Sedgewick, shifting them from reactive researchers to proactive ghost-catching entrepreneurs. This is their point of no return—they prove the business works and commit fully to being Ghostbusters, raising the stakes as they become public figures.

climax.description: Peck demands a cop arrest the Ghostbusters but the cop flees as the Stay-Puft Man climbs the building, and the Ghostbusters fire at him causing him to burn and fall, then they cross the streams into the gate causing an explosion.

validation.ruling: Scene #132 is the transformation and revelation of Gozer, but not the peak confrontation. Scene #135 (p90, 96%) is the true climax: the Ghostbusters battle the Stay-Puft Marshmallow Man and cross their proton streams into the gate, causing the massive explosion that defeats Gozer. This is the highest tension, the ultimate confrontation where they risk everything, and the resolution of the main story question.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW YORK PUBLIC LIBRARY - DAY
scenes.summary: The exterior of the New York Public Library is shown on a sunny day with people on the steps and hustlers in the park.

scenes.heading: INT. MAIN READING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: A librarian circulates quietly through the peaceful main reading room while an unseen presence watches her from above.

scenes.heading: INT. WORK AREA
scenes.summary: The librarian reads a book in a back room while card catalogue drawers silently open behind her and index cards shoot out unnoticed.

scenes.heading: INT. THE NEXT AISLE - DAY
scenes.summary: The librarian rounds a corner, sees the thing that has been watching her, and screams in horror.

scenes.heading: EXT. COLUMBIA UNIVERSITY - DAY
scenes.summary: Students enter and leave Weaver Hall at Columbia University.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT - WEAVER HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: The exterior of the Paranormal Studies Laboratory door is shown with graffiti and a maid sign.

scenes.heading: INT. PARANORMAL STUDIES LAB - SAME TIME - DAY
scenes.summary: Dr. Venkman administers an ESP test, shocking the male volunteer for wrong answers while flirting with the female volunteer and praising all her guesses regardless of accuracy.

scenes.heading: EXT. PUBLIC LIBRARY - DAY
scenes.summary: Venkman and Stantz arrive at the library by taxi, with Stantz explaining their psychic occurrence research while Venkman expresses skepticism about ghost hunting.

scenes.heading: INT. MAIN READING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Spengler uses a stethoscope on a table listening for spirits when Venkman arrives and startles him by slamming a book down.

scenes.heading: INT. AN OFFICE - A LITTLE LATER
scenes.summary: Venkman questions the librarian about the spirit she saw while a paramedic treats her for shock, and she describes it reaching for her with arms.

scenes.heading: INT. DEEP IN THE STACKS - DAY
scenes.summary: The team moves through dark stacks with monitoring equipment as bookshelves topple over, nearly crushing them before they jump to safety.

scenes.heading: INT. THE DARK AISLE - DAY
scenes.summary: The team observes an ethereal presence forming into an elderly woman between the stacks, and Venkman awkwardly attempts to communicate before the ghost turns hostile and chases them.
scenes.turningPointNote: First genuine ghost encounter — validates their research and sets the supernatural threat in motion

scenes.heading: EXT. COLUMBIA UNIVERSITY - DAY.
scenes.summary: Venkman, Stantz, and Spengler walk back to their lab arguing about their failed attempt to capture the ghost, with Stantz excited about the encounter despite the chaos.

scenes.heading: EXT. WEAVER HALL - A LITTLE LATER
scenes.summary: Venkman gives Spengler a candy bar as they enter Weaver Hall, taking back his previous criticisms.

scenes.heading: INT. WEAVER HALL~ DAY
scenes.summary: The three walk through the hall discussing the Nobel Prize, with Venkman claiming credit for introducing Stantz and Spengler.

scenes.heading: INT. THE BASEMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: A workman scrapes their names off the lab door with a razor blade as they enter.

scenes.heading: INT. THE LAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Dean Yaeger informs them that the Board of Regents has terminated their grant and they must vacate immediately due to lack of results.
scenes.turningPointNote: Forced out of academia — pushes them toward starting their own business

scenes.heading: EXT. COLUMBIA UNIVERSITY - DAY - A LITTLE LATER
scenes.summary: Stantz and Venkman sit desolate on a bench discussing their ruined academic reputations and uncertain future.

scenes.heading: EXT. WIDE ANGLE VIEW OF MANHATTAN - DAY
scenes.summary: A wide angle view of Manhattan is shown during the day.

scenes.heading: EXT. AVENUE OF THE AMERICAS - DAY
scenes.summary: The three emerge from Irving Trust headquarters after Stantz has mortgaged his family house at nineteen percent interest to fund their venture.
scenes.turningPointNote: Financial commitment made — they're now fully invested in the Ghostbusters business

scenes.heading: EXT. FIREBALL - DAY
scenes.summary: Venkman stands with a real estate woman in the garage bay of an abandoned firehouse.

scenes.heading: INT. GARAGE BAY - DAY
scenes.summary: The real estate woman shows Venkman the firehouse space while Spengler corrects her square footage and Stantz excitedly tests the fire pole.

scenes.heading: EXT. UPPER WEST SIDE - DAY
scenes.summary: A panoramic view shows heavy traffic on Central Park West on a sunny day.

scenes.heading: EXT. 78th AND CENTRAL PARK WEST - DAY
scenes.summary: Dana Barrett walks up to an unusual Gothic high-rise carrying a cello case and groceries, ignoring men on the street.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH-RISE APARTMENT BUILDING - LOBBY - DAY
scenes.summary: Dana crosses the lobby and gets into the elevator.

scenes.heading: INT. THIRTY FIFTH FLOOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Dana's neighbor Louis Tully intercepts her at her apartment door, awkwardly trying to engage her in conversation about exercise and mineral water before she politely declines and enters her apartment.

scenes.heading: INT. CHILDREN'S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A commercial shows the Ghostbusters successfully removing a ghost from children's bedroom and being thanked by a grateful family.

scenes.heading: INT. CHILDREN'S BEDROOM
scenes.summary: Stantz emerges from under a bed announcing he caught the ghost while Venkman hands the father a bill and the family thanks them.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRONT DOOR
scenes.summary: The family waves goodbye and sings the Ghostbusters jingle as Dana turns off the TV and goes into the kitchen.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN
scenes.summary: Dana unpacks groceries when eggs erupt from their carton and fry on the counter without heat, then a demonic voice roars her name from inside the refrigerator.
scenes.turningPointNote: Dana's supernatural encounter — brings the main characters and the central threat together

scenes.heading: EXT. FIREHALL - DAY
scenes.summary: Stantz drives up to the firehouse in a battered 1959 Cadillac ambulance he purchased for fourteen hundred dollars.

scenes.heading: INT. GARAGE BAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Stantz shows Venkman the ambulance while Janine Melnitz works at reception and flirts with Spengler as he wires the phone system.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Dana, hooked to a lie detector, tells Venkman and Stantz that something in her refrigerator is trying to get her, and Spengler confirms she believes she's telling the truth.

scenes.heading: INT. DANA'S APARTMENT - LATER THAT AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Dana leads Venkman into her darkened apartment and they discuss whether she should move out, with Venkman pretending to know about classical music.

scenes.heading: INT. THE KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Venkman inspects Dana's messy kitchen with his monitoring device, breaks the refrigerator handle, finds nothing inside, and tells Dana there's no sign of unusual activity.

scenes.heading: EXT. HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Venkman stands with his nose against Dana's closed door, then leaves as Louis Tully peeks out jealously and accidentally locks himself out trying to grab his newspaper.

scenes.heading: INT. FIREHALL KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Venkman returns to the firehouse where Stantz and Spengler eat Chinese food, and he expresses worry about their cash-flow problem since they haven't encountered any ghosts.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOTEL SEDGEWICK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Doormen help people in and out of vehicles under the Hotel Sedgewick's elegant awning at night.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A do not disturb sign hangs on Room 1210 with soft music coming from inside.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 1210 - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A newlywed couple makes love in a dark hotel room, then the groom insults the bride and turns away as a green vapor materializes and devours food from the room service cart.

scenes.heading: INT. FIREHALL OFFICE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Janine answers a phone call from the Hotel Sedgewick about a ghost and hits the alarm button to summon the team.
scenes.turningPointNote: First paying customer — the business becomes real

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The alarm sounds in the kitchen and everyone jumps up, scattering takeout cartons.

scenes.heading: INT. DORM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The team scrambles into jumpsuits and slides down the brass pole, with Stantz hitting it at the wrong angle.

scenes.heading: INT. GARAGE BAY - EQUIPMENT RACKS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The team grabs equipment and the ambulance, now painted black with strobes and modified siren, screeches out of the firehouse.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOTEL - FRONT DOOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Ghostbusters' ambulance arrives at the hotel entrance with siren blaring as they leap out and remove equipment while people stop to stare.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL LOBBY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Ghostbusters enter the hotel lobby in their futuristic jumpsuits and proton packs as the agitated manager greets them and explains guests are starting to ask questions.

scenes.heading: INT. THE ELEVATOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The nervous Ghostbusters ride the elevator up, realizing they've never had a successful equipment test, then cautiously exit onto the twelfth floor.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 1210 - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The team enters Room 1210 and takes readings, detecting that something was there and used the air vents, while Venkman complains about their lack of success.

scenes.heading: INT. A CORRIDOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Spengler checks doors in a corridor and a woman in a towel answers, sarcastically responding to his question about being in the bathroom.

scenes.heading: INT. ANOTHER CORRIDOR - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Stantz's PKE meter spikes, he turns a corner and freezes in terror upon seeing the translucent yellow vapor hunched over a room service cart feeding on scraps.

scenes.heading: INT. A HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A man in a hotel room watches as noxious vapor shoots through his wall, blows over him, and exits through the air vent, turning his white dinner jacket yellow and brown.

scenes.heading: INT. A CORRIDOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Venkman stands alone in a hotel hallway muttering about working for Ghostbusters when his PKE meter beeps and he hears rattling dishes approaching from down the hall.

scenes.heading: INT. STAIRWELL - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Stantz runs down a stairwell three steps at a time as Venkman's screams crackle over the radio.

scenes.heading: INT. THIRD FLOOR
scenes.summary: Stantz finds Venkman flat on his back covered in yellow-green ectofluid, screaming that it slimed him, then Spengler radios that the ghost is in the Banquet Room.

scenes.heading: INT. BANQUET ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Ghostbusters enter the banquet room, spot the vapor on the ceiling, and fire their proton streams at it, destroying the chandelier and blasting chunks of plaster but missing the dodging ghost.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: The Hotel Manager and Maintenance Man nervously listen to the sounds of heavy property damage coming from the banquet room.

scenes.heading: INT. BANQUET ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Ghostbusters maneuver the vapor into the center of the room, Spengler deploys his trap with a foot pedal, and they successfully capture the ghost.
scenes.turningPointNote: First successful ghost capture — validates their business and technology

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Ghostbusters emerge from the elevator with the trap, and Stantz excitedly tells the Hotel Manager they caught a Class Five Full Roaming Vapor.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOTEL SEDGEWICK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Ghostbusters exit the hotel to find a mob scene with crowds, police barricades, and reporters firing questions at them as Stantz holds up the trap and announces they got one.
scenes.turningPointNote: Public breakthrough — Ghostbusters become a media phenomenon

scenes.heading: EXT. TIMES SQUARE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: News coverage shows the Ghostbusters' fame spreading across the Eastern Seaboard with multiple paranormal sightings reported and Roy Brady reporting from outside their firehouse.

scenes.heading: INT. ECTOMOBILE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Stantz drives the Ectomobile while Spengler and Venkman sleep exhausted in their seats as Joe Franklin interviews Stantz on TV about ghost-themed movies.

scenes.heading: INT. RADIO STUDIO
scenes.summary: Larry King hosts a radio phone-in show debating whether the Ghostbusters caused the ghost sightings and whether they should be allowed to carry unlicensed proton mass drivers.

scenes.heading: EXT. LINCOLN CENTER - DAY
scenes.summary: Dana exits Lincoln Center with a violinist, sees Venkman waiting by the fountain, and crosses to talk with him while he compliments her rehearsal and asks her to dinner.

scenes.heading: INT. RECEPTION AREA - DAY
scenes.summary: Janine handles a barrage of phone calls telling callers the Ghostbusters are completely booked until a week from Friday while Winston Zeddemore fills out a job application.

scenes.heading: EXT. FIREHALL - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Autograph hounds wait outside the firehouse as Ecto One pulls in beneath a new neon Ghostbusters logo sign.

scenes.heading: INT. GARAGE BAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Venkman and Stantz return exhausted and slime-covered from a Brooklyn job, Venkman drops the paid invoice on Janine's desk, and Stantz reviews tonight's calls before introducing Winston as their new employee.

scenes.heading: INT. THE OFFICE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Walter Peck from the EPA questions Venkman about his credentials and the storage facility, but Venkman refuses to let him see it and asks him to leave, prompting Peck to threaten to return with a court order.
scenes.turningPointNote: EPA antagonist introduced — regulatory threat to their operation established

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Dana tiptoes past Louis' loud party but he rushes out and insists she come in, mentioning he made nachos with non-fat cheese.

scenes.heading: INT. DANA'S APARTMENT - NI GHT
scenes.summary: Dana enters her apartment exhausted, sits down, removes her leg warmers, and answers a phone call from her mother reassuring her that everything is fine.

scenes.heading: EXT. DANA'S ROOFTOP - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A bright flash shoots from the pyramid on Dana's rooftop, and both stone Terror Dogs are now missing from the temple structure.
scenes.turningPointNote: Supernatural threat escalates — Terror Dogs animate and leave their posts

scenes.heading: INT. LOUIS' APARTMENT - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Louis hosts his party, cleaning ashtrays with Windex and offering generic acetylsalicylic acid to a guest with a headache while the buffet table displays his accountant-themed food.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Louis enters his bedroom and tosses coats onto the bed, unknowingly landing them on a live, bristling Terror Dog standing on all fours.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Louis exits the bedroom as a tremendous roar freezes the guests, then the Terror Dog bursts through the door, lands in the center of the room snarling, and Louis runs for the exit as it lunges after him.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Louis runs down the hallway bellowing as the Terror Dog bursts through the door and chases him, and Louis ducks into the elevator.

scenes.heading: EXT. DANA'S BUILDING - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Louis runs out of the building screaming about a bear in his apartment, crosses the street into the park, and the Terror Dog runs over the doorman and bounds after him.

scenes.heading: EXT. CENTRAL PARK
scenes.summary: Louis runs past two bums discussing boxing and karate, screaming for them to run, and the Terror Dog streaks past them in pursuit.

scenes.heading: EXT. CENTRAL PARK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Louis runs through a tunnel, sees the lights of Tavern on the Green restaurant, and heads straight for it.

scenes.heading: EXT. TAVERN ON THE GREEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Louis runs into the restaurant courtyard, tries multiple locked doors, hears a growl from the bushes, and desperately pounds on the glass.

scenes.heading: INT. TAVERN ON THE GREEN - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Wealthy restaurant patrons look up and see Louis pounding on the glass screaming for help, then ignore his pleas and return to their meal.

scenes.heading: EXT. COURTYARD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Louis turns to face the advancing Terror Dog in the courtyard, trembles and says "nice doggie," and the creature pounces with a tremendous roar.
scenes.turningPointNote: Louis is possessed by the Terror Dog — Keymaster secured

scenes.heading: INT. TAVERN ON THE GREEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The roar echoes through the restaurant followed by sounds of violent struggle, then Louis walks out of the park with a glazed look and tells a horse he is Vinz Clortho, Keymaster of Gozer.

scenes.heading: EXT. DANA ' S APARTMENT BUILDING - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Venkman arrives at Dana's building with flowers, sees cops talking to disheveled party guests about a cougar going berserk, and heads to the elevators.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Venkman rings Dana's bell, she opens the door radically changed with flowing hair, naked shoulders, love-slave eyes, and tattered clothes, staring at him with supernatural longing.
scenes.turningPointNote: Dana is possessed by Zuul — Gatekeeper secured

scenes.heading: INT. DANA'S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Venkman steers possessed Dana onto the bed, checks her pulse and pupils while she demands he take her body, and he questions her trying to speak to Dana while she insists she is Zuul the Gatekeeper.

scenes.heading: EXT. HUDSON PARKWAY - SAME NIGHT
scenes.summary: Winston drives the Ectomobile while Stantz examines blueprints of Dana's building, Winston asks if Stantz is Christian, and Stantz reacts to something unusual in the structural ironwork.

scenes.heading: EXT. FORT DETMERRING - NIGHT - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Stantz and Winston arrive at Fort Detmerring where a Park Ranger explains they've had a paranormal problem for quite some time and called the Ghostbusters weeks ago.

scenes.heading: INT. FORT - GUNNER'S CORRIDOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Stantz and Winston walk through the fort's gunner's corridor wearing their equipment, split up to check different areas, and their footsteps fade as they leave frame.

scenes.heading: EXT. FIREHALL - NIGHT - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: A police van arrives at the firehouse, Spengler meets the sergeant who explains they picked up a guy who's too weird for Bellevue or lockup, and the sergeant opens the van door.

scenes.heading: INT. THE POLICE VAN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Louis sits in the police van wearing a strait jacket and restraints, asks if Spengler is the Gatekeeper, and Spengler notes the strange glow in his eyes and orders him brought inside.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT - LATER
scenes.summary: Louis is hooked up to monitoring equipment with electrodes on his head while Spengler questions him, and Louis declares he is Vinz Clortho, Keymaster of Gozer, waiting inside Louis Tully's body for the sign.

scenes.heading: INT. DANA'S BEDROOM - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Venkman calls Spengler from Dana's bedroom where she floats three feet off the bed asleep, Spengler reveals he has the Keymaster, and after a long silence Venkman tells him not to let the two of them meet.

scenes.heading: INT. FORT DETMERRING - SINGLE OFFICERS' QUARTERS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Stantz tries on a period military uniform in the restored officers' quarters, models it in the mirror, tests the bed, lies down with his hands behind his head, and falls asleep.

scenes.heading: INT. BARRACKS CORRIDOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Winston walks down the barracks corridor, hears voices, asks if Stantz is okay, and Stantz yells from inside for him to come back later.

scenes.heading: EXT. FIREHALL - MORNING
scenes.summary: Multiple official vehicles including police cars, a government K-car, a Con Edison van, and a sheriff's car pull up to the firehouse, and Peck enters with cops and a police captain.

scenes.heading: INT. FIREHALL - RECEPTION AREA
scenes.summary: Peck and the police walk past Janine toward the basement, she blocks their way demanding a warrant, and Peck hands her cease and desist orders along with a seizure writ.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT - LATER
scenes.summary: Spengler argues with Peck in the basement while Louis watches, Peck demands to see what's in the storage facility, and Spengler offers to show him the monitor but Peck ignores it.

scenes.heading: EXT. FIREBALL
scenes.summary: Venkman arrives in a taxi, sees the police cars parked outside, and dashes into the firehouse.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT
scenes.summary: Venkman tries to cooperate but Peck refuses, Spengler warns that Peck wants to shut down the storage grid, Venkman says they won't be responsible for consequences, and Peck orders the Con Edison man to shut it off.
scenes.turningPointNote: Storage grid shutdown ordered — all captured ghosts about to be released

scenes.heading: EXT. THE FIREHALL
scenes.summary: Everyone runs out of the firehouse as the storage facility explodes, shooting a powerful geyser of iridescent energy a hundred feet into the air.
scenes.turningPointNote: Containment breach — all ghosts released into New York City

scenes.heading: INT. DANA'S BEDROOM - THAT INSTANT
scenes.summary: Dana's eyes pop open in her bedroom as if cued by the distant explosion rumbling.

scenes.heading: EXT. FIREHALL - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Venkman, Spengler and others dodge debris from the energy geyser while Louis, in a trance-like state, walks away unnoticed after declaring it is a sign.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOWER MANHATTAN
scenes.summary: An iridescent cloud from the geyser moves across Manhattan toward Dana's building as emergency vehicles race through the city.

scenes.heading: EXT. FIREHALL - LATER
scenes.summary: Spengler answers questions from fire officials and a hazmat expert about the translucent blue substance spurting from the firehall roof.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE BOWERY
scenes.summary: Louis walks purposefully uptown while a crowd flees back up from a subway entrance pursued by an apparition.

scenes.heading: INT. DANA'S APARTMENT
scenes.summary: Dana stands at her open bedroom window gazing down at the streets below.

scenes.heading: EXT. SEDGEWICK HOTEL - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: The Onion-Headed Vapor rises from a hot dog vendor's cart and floats away with the cart following under its own power.

scenes.heading: EXT. EXXON BUILDING - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: A businessman rushes out of the Exxon Building and jumps into a cab.

scenes.heading: INT. THE BACK SEAT
scenes.summary: The businessman shouts his destination and sees through the partition that the cab driver is a decomposed corpse.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE STREET
scenes.summary: The corpse-driven cab speeds away and turns the wrong way up a one-way street.

scenes.heading: INT. AN OFFICE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: An invisible entity gooses and tickles a female programmer in an office, forcing her to walk briskly toward the ladies room.

scenes.heading: EXT. CENTRAL PARK - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Louis enters a tunnel where muggers confront him, but when he asks if they are the Gatekeeper, they flee in terror from something behind him.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET
scenes.summary: The corpse-driven cab weaves through traffic at breakneck speed, forcing cars onto the curb and pedestrians to run.

scenes.heading: INT. THE BACK SEAT
scenes.summary: The businessman reads the Wall Street Journal in the back seat, oblivious to the dangerous driving.

scenes.heading: EXT. ST. MARKS PLAYHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: A graffiti-covered movie theater advertises an all-night horror show in 3-D.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER
scenes.summary: A packed theater audience wearing 3-D glasses heckles a bad horror film until a piercing supernatural whine builds and ghosts materialize throughout the theater.

scenes.heading: INT. NYPD LOCK-UP
scenes.summary: Winston worries about prison time while Venkman, Stantz and Spengler study blueprints of Dana's building, discovering its roof structure resembles a NASA telemetry tracker as their cellmates listen intently.

scenes.heading: EXT. CITY HALL
scenes.summary: The Ghostbusters are hustled past shouting reporters into City Hall.

scenes.heading: INT. MAYOR'S OFFICE
scenes.summary: Secretaries and aides stop to stare as the Ghostbusters are led through the chaotic outer offices.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE OF THE MAYOR OF NEW YORK CITY
scenes.summary: The Mayor and city officials argue until the Ghostbusters arrive, then Peck accuses them of fraud while Venkman explains the supernatural threat and warns that shutting down their containment system caused the disaster.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Ghostbusters convince the Mayor to let them confront Gozer, shifting from arrested criminals to the city's only hope.

scenes.heading: EXT. DANA'S APARTMENT BUILDING - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Louis arrives at Dana's building as strange lights flash from windows, enters, and a huge tremor shakes the structure.

scenes.heading: INT. DANA'S FLOOR
scenes.summary: Louis exits the elevator through panicked tenants, encounters his neighbor Mrs. Blum who urges him to evacuate, but he declares the Traveller is coming and enters Dana's apartment.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOADING DOCK
scenes.summary: Winston and Spengler charge the proton packs while a police captain reports the building is cleared, and Janine gives Spengler a lucky coin.

scenes.heading: INT. ECTOMOBILE
scenes.summary: Venkman rallies his team as professionals and signals for them to move out.

scenes.heading: EXT. REAR OF CITY HALL
scenes.summary: A police motorcade with the Ectomobile speeds uptown past National Guard trucks.

scenes.heading: EXT. DANA'S APARTMENT BUILDING
scenes.summary: A massive crowd including media, doomsday freaks, priests, punks and Hassidic Jews watches Dana's building as the Ectomobile arrives to huge cheers.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE ROOFTOP - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Dana and Louis emerge onto the rooftop, approach the Temple and mount pedestals as the Temple walls begin to vibrate.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE STREET BELOW
scenes.summary: Blinding light flashes from all windows as the Ghostbusters trudge up thirty-five flights of stairs.

scenes.heading: INT. THIRTY-FIFTH FLOOR HALLWAY
scenes.summary: The Ghostbusters stagger exhausted into the thirty-fifth floor hallway.

scenes.heading: INT. DANA'S DOOR
scenes.summary: Venkman rings Dana's doorbell and the door falls off its hinges, revealing the damaged apartment.

scenes.heading: INT. DANA'S APARTMENT
scenes.summary: The Ghostbusters see the exterior wall is completely gone and the apartment is destroyed by wind and damage.

scenes.heading: INT. THE KITCHEN
scenes.summary: The team discovers the refrigerator blown away, revealing a stone staircase, and Venkman orders Stantz to lead them up.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE ROOF
scenes.summary: Dana and Louis transform into Terror Dogs as the Temple opens to reveal a suspended pyramid, then Gozer materializes on the pyramid and the Terror Dogs attack the Ghostbusters.
scenes.turningPointNote: Gozer manifests physically and the final confrontation begins as Dana and Louis complete their transformation into Terror Dogs.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE EAVES
scenes.summary: Cement carvings and moldings crack and break off the edge of the roof.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE STREET
scenes.summary: The crowd flees as debris falls, then Gozer speaks demanding they choose the form of the destructor, and Stantz accidentally thinks of the Stay-Puft Marshmallow Man which materializes.
scenes.turningPointNote: Stantz's thought inadvertently determines the form of the destructor, creating the Stay-Puft Marshmallow Man and escalating the threat.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE STREET
scenes.summary: Peck demands a cop arrest the Ghostbusters but the cop flees as the Stay-Puft Man climbs the building, and the Ghostbusters fire at him causing him to burn and fall, then they cross the streams into the gate causing an explosion.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Ghostbusters cross the streams despite the danger, destroying Gozer's gate and ending the supernatural threat in the climactic moment.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - NEAR THE APARTMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Two bums inspect a massive mound of cooling marshmallow in the street and speculate about why someone would dump it there.
